core_mental_model: A tool is a capability grant; scope by least-privilege. Tool definition (reuse) and tool binding (per-agent, minimal) are separate decisions.

operation_candidates.name: Least-privilege role->tool mapping
operation_candidates.trigger: Assembling a multi-agent crew/graph from a shared tool pool
operation_candidates.action: Start each agent at tools=[]; add a tool only when the role requires that agent to exercise it; reuse definition, bind per-role
operation_candidates.output: Minimal per-agent tools list + binding matrix

operation_candidates.name: Side-effect tool guarding
operation_candidates.trigger: A tool performs a write or destructive operation
operation_candidates.action: Bind to one agent only; add HITL/approval guard before the effect; make op idempotent

operation_candidates.name: Shared-registry vs per-agent binding decision
operation_candidates.trigger: A tool registry plus N agents; must decide wiring
operation_candidates.action: Share at definition layer; decide binding per-agent via OP-1; never registry.all() on every agent
operation_candidates.output: One definition source, N minimal per-agent bindings

operation_candidates.name: Per-agent tool-count limit
operation_candidates.trigger: Agent tool list grows >=8 or starts mis-selecting tools
operation_candidates.action: Cap ~8 tools/agent; split role, add routing sub-agent, or demote read-only tools to context
operation_candidates.output: Every agent <= ceiling; better selection accuracy
operation_candidates.rationale: too many tools degrades selection by same mechanism as too many agents degrades coordination

operation_candidates.name: Zero-tool synthesis agents
operation_candidates.action: Bind no tools; feed via task context / state
operation_candidates.output: Tool-free agent that cannot wander into capabilities

operation_candidates.name: Binding-matrix audit
operation_candidates.action: Build agents x tools matrix; flag write/destructive bound to >1 agent or unguarded; flag agents over count limit

operation_candidates.name: Tighten scope as fastest misuse fix
operation_candidates.trigger: Agent observed using a tool outside its role
operation_candidates.action: Remove the tool from that agent's binding rather than prompt-engineering 'do not use X'

dilemma_cases.title: Shared tool registry vs per-agent tool sets
The 20-tool agent that picks the wrong tool
Should a destructive tool ever be shared across agents?

anti_patterns: blanket tool sharing (tools=[all] on every agent)
prompt-patching tool misuse instead of removing the binding
sharing the destructive tool itself instead of its definition
